market_summary: Mixed session. S&P -0.15%, Dow -0.48%, Nasdaq +0.12%. PPI surged 6% YoY (Iran war energy costs). Tech outperformed on Nvidia/AMD/MU strength. Trump arrived in Beijing for Xi summit (May 14-15). Markets weighing inflation spike vs trade detente hopes. Gold fell to $4,690 (-0.5%), silver $85.44 (-1.1%) as hot inflation killed rate-cut expectations. BTC held ~$80.5K, ETH ~$2,250. Oil steadied around WTI $102, Brent $107.
key_events_tomorrow: Trump-Xi summit Day 1 in Beijing (May 14) — trade deal progress, tariff discussions
Business delegations: Musk, Cook, Fink, Boeing CEO in China — potential purchase agreements
Continued Iran-US ceasefire/Strait of Hormuz monitoring
overnight_risks: Trump-Xi summit headlines could whipsaw markets — any tariff surprise or Iran mediation news
Asian markets reacting to US PPI inflation surge and rate-cut repricing
Iran ceasefire on "massive life support" per Trump — Strait of Hormuz disruption risk remains
India raised gold/silver import tariffs to 15% from 6% — demand dampener for precious metals
UAE departure from OPEC effective May 1 — oil supply dynamics shifting

instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver $85.44, down 1.1%. Hot inflation killing rate-cut hopes pressures metals. But COT positioning still bullish, central banks buying 700-900t gold/yr, BofA target $135-309 silver. Industrial demand (solar/EV/AI) intact. India tariff hike (15%) dampens short-term demand.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold $4,690, down 0.5%. JP Morgan targets $5K by Q4 2026, SSGA sees structural bull to $5K. Central bank buying 16th straight year. ETF flows mixed — US outflows but global record AUM $701B. Producer COT positions bullish.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC $80.5K, steady. RSI 50, consolidation regime. Trump-Xi summit adding uncertainty. BitMEX Hayes predicts $126K rally on AI spending + geopolitics. Inverse H&S pattern targeting $84K.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: ETH $2,250, down 2.8% from Tuesday. Golden Cross pattern targeting $2,680. Still lagging BTC. ETH/BTC ratio compressed.
instrument_news.MSTR: MSTR ~$178, 818K BTC treasury ($61.8B, avg cost $75.5K). Q1 revenue +11.9% YoY. Major shift: will sell BTC for preferred stock dividends (ending "never sell" approach). Stock +40% past month. Analyst consensus "Strong Buy", target range $176-228.

metals_outlook: Short-term bearish pressure from hot inflation killing rate-cut expectations. India tariff hike dampens demand. But structural bull thesis intact: central banks buying record pace, COT bullish, gold targeting $5K by Q4, silver has 150%+ gain past year. Trump-Xi summit could reduce geopolitical premium if Iran mediation progresses. Key: silver 3d consensus accuracy 57.4% — better than 1d (49.7%), suggesting medium-term signals more reliable than daily noise.
oil_outlook: WTI $102, Brent $107. Iran ceasefire on life support. Saudi warns 100M bbl/week supply loss. IEA: global inventories falling 8.5M b/d in Q2. UAE left OPEC May 1. EIA expects Brent to drop to $89/b Q4 2026 as production normalizes. Key risk: Strait of Hormuz closure duration.
sentiment_shift: Risk-off tilt. Hot PPI + Iran uncertainty + rate-cut repricing = cautious positioning. But Trump-Xi summit creates potential positive catalyst. Tech resilience suggests selective risk appetite remains. Overall: neutral-to-cautious for Thursday.
